title: Yoga on the Farm
description:
Outdoor Yoga on the Farm
Stretch, breathe, and reconnect—surrounded by the beauty of the farm. Join us for a midweek reset where movement meets nature: soft grass under your feet, fresh air in your lungs, and the rhythms of the land guiding your flow. Whether you’re new to yoga or a seasoned practitioner, this welcoming outdoor class invites you to slow down, tune in, and cultivate a sense of balance—both on and off the mat.



		
	



	🗓️ Every Wednesday, April 22 - June 3
⏰ 4-5 p.m.
🎟️ $10 per class


			
		
			
			Sign Up		
			
	


	Know before you go: 

Dress in layers in case it's very warm or begins to get chilly
If you forget your mat, no worries! We will have plenty for you to choose from
Don't forget your sunscreen and reusable water bottle.
Optional: Bring blocks or a towel

Instructor: 
Julie Snyder Block has over 25 years of yoga practice and holds a 200-hour RYT yoga teacher certification. She loves teaching a Vinyasa-style yoga that blends breath, movement, and mindfulnes

title: Havdalah on the Farm
description:
Havdalah on the Farm
Havdalah, literally meaning separation, is a ritual held at the end of Shabbat, after the sun has set and when three stars appear. There are blessings over wine, a candle, and spices. It is a ritual that allows us to hold onto the sweet moments of Shabbat for just a few more minutes before going into our busy week.
Join Coastal Roots Farm and The Hive on Saturday, February 19th from 7 - 8:30 PM on the Farm for a special Havdalah service led by Sharone Oren and Ashly Tamayo.
In honor of Black History month we will be watching "Blewish" a short film about a Black Jewish child, discuss Black Jewish identities and have an interactive experience while diving deeper into how we can uplift Black history and support Black communities as a Jewish community.
Community members of all ages and backgrounds are invited! 
Masks encouraged. Pre-registration only.


			
		
			
			Click here to register!

title: Earth Day on the Farm
description:
At Coastal Roots Farm, we believe that Earth Day is everyday! We prioritize farming techniques that build and maintain soil and sequester carbon, not just to grow great food but to contribute toward a solution for the larger problem of climate change provide equitable environmental education to people of all ages, and locally grown, organic food to our community and those who need it the most.
Join us on Sunday, April 24th from 9:30-11:30am for a day of volunteering and celebrating the earth! People of all background and ages welcome. Registration is a suggested donation of $18/adult, $9/child.
Volunteer tasks include: 10am-planting, 10:30am-spreading compost and mulch, and 11am pruning passionfruit vines. There will also be additional craft stations with Earth Day related activities for younger guests.
We recommend wearing clothes toed shoes, a hat, and bringing a reusable water bottle.


			
		
			
			Click Here to Register!

description:

title: Rosh Hashanah on the Farm
description:
Rosh Hashanah, the Jewish new year, is a fall holiday that celebrates the completion of another year and serves as a time of rejoice and introspection. Gather with us to celebrate the new year, reflect together, and prepare for what is to come.
Geared towards young families, especially good for ages 2-10.
September 25th 9:30-11:30am
Suggested donation: $18/adult, $9/child
Activities include:

Taking a closer look at bees and the process of how honey makes it from hive to bottle with Kendal Sager.
Completing Farm chores and supporting the maintenance of the Education Farm and Garden spaces by "Starting a new cycle for the new year."
Harvesting and creating a sweet snack.
Holiday and nature-inspired crafts
Experience a mini tashlich: recognizing past mistakes and thinking about good deeds we want to do in this coming Jewish year.

title: Natural Dye Workshop for Teens
description:
Natural dyes can be made from materials such as leaves, bark, flowers, fruit, minerals, and more! Creating dyes from natural materials cuts out the use of harsh chemicals that can be harmful to people and the planet. 
Learn how to make natural dyes using materials found at home or in your yard to tie dye a bandana to take home! Led by Aliza Zollman, a passionate student who has been moved by the environmental impacts of the textile industry and fast fashion.
For teens ages 11-18 only.
Sunday, November 20th
11:00am - 12:30pm
“Aliza Zollman is a Gold Award applicant, holding workshops about natural dyes and sustainable fashion for other teenagers. She learned to sew while in elementary school, but became interested in slow fashion in middle school after learning about the child labor industry. Aliza attends San Diego High School.”

title: Fiddler Sing-Along
description:
In honor of the first night of Chanukah, join us for a sing-along celebration of the beloved film Fiddler on the Roof!
In partnership with our neighbors at The Hive, we are excited to announce an evening on the Farm filled with tasty snacks, warm drinks, dress up, and a whole lot of singing. Enjoy root veggie latkes (with Farm veggies) made fresh by The Hive's Chef Tiffani, along with apple cider to keep you warm. There will be ample heat lamp and blankets. The film has subtitles for all songs, making it easy to join in on the singing and fun! No good voice necessary – come ready to sing loud and proud and just as you are.
Guests are encouraged to bring their own blankets, chairs, as well as food and beverage. Dressing up as your favorite Fiddler character encouraged!
December 18th, 5-9pm
$18/adult $36/family

title: Tishrei Ag Fest
description:
The cycle of agriculture requires that we continually regenerate, repair, and renew in order to grow. Likewise, the Hebrew month of Tishrei, often referred to as the “month of beginning,” is the first month of the Jewish year and reminds us that such renewal is possible at the human level too: with each new year, we reflect where we have been and where we’re going so that we may begin again with intention.
The month of Tishrei encompasses the Jewish holidays known as “the chaggim (holidays)” – Rosh Hashanah, Yom Kippur, Sukkot, and Simchat Torah. The start of the month is marked by the sliver of a new moon that grows increasingly bright with each passing day.
Embracing the light of our beloved Sukkot Harvest Festival, Coastal Roots Farm’s first-ever Tishrei Ag Fest will also elevate these other holidays and invite us to kick off the joyous and regenerative beginning of this Jewish year.
Tishrei Ag Fest 2023
A purposeful alternative to our Sukkot Harvest Festival!
"Begin Again"

title: Farm Film & Music Series: Tribal Waters
description:
Join us this summer for a 5-part series featuring inspiring documentary films, local musicians, and meaningful conversations around equitable food systems, environmental justice, and caring for the planet.
Thursday, September 28th - Tribal Waters directed by Jon Klaczkiewicz, a film by Teton Gravity Research presented by Patagonia, documents the course and history of the magnificent Wind River, now the home waters of the Eastern Shoshone and Northern Arapaho Tribes. The story of the Wind is a record of stolen ancestral lands, insatiable agricultural demands and a mercurial, unreliable American justice system.
6pm Doors open. Enjoy live music by Anne:Rice and tabling with local organizations.
7:30pm Screening: Tribal Waters
All people welcome. Guests are encouraged to bring their own blankets, chairs, food, and drinks to enjoy the evening on the Farm.
Please leave your furry pets at home, we are a pet-free facility!
Admission $10/person.
We are incredibly grateful to our Presenting Spon

title: Queer in the Sukkah – Preregistration is required. No walk-ups will be allowed
description:

You are invited to Coastal Roots Farm’s first-ever queer gathering and party in celebration of Sukkot, the Jewish Harvest Festival sometimes referred to as "the time of our joy."
Soak in the magic of our farm; experience joyful Sukkot traditions; get inspired by Hanz Enyeart, Queer Jewish scholar and writer (check out his column in The Rage magazine!) who will invite in the stories of little-known Queer Jewish history-makers; eat, drink, and socialize with new friends as the sun sets.
All are welcome; no need to identify as Jewish to attend!

title: SoCal Tribal Food Sovereignty Gathering
description:
The SoCal Tribal Food Sovereignty Gathering at Coastal Roots Farm is an opportunity for Tribal members in Southern California that are actively cultivating or interested in joining the food sovereignty movement among Indigenous Nations. Examples of people active in food sovereignty work include Tribal farmers, seed keepers, land stewards, hunters, livestock owners, foragers, botanists, fishers, medicine makers, and cooks.
The Gathering will include learning sessions, networking between Tribal community members, and shared meals provided by the Farm. This event is intended for Tribal community members and those working directly with Tribal communities.
Sunday, October 21st from 9:30am - 3pm
Participants are encouraged to carpool and transportation scholarships are available upon request! To request transportation support to/from the Gathering at Coastal Roots Farm, please note the request in the "additional information" section of the registration form or email megan@coastalrootsfarm.or

title: Outdoor Mandala Making Workshop – Circles of Love
description:
Mandala is Sanskrit for “circle” and is most commonly associated with Hindu and Buddhist cultures.
Join the Farm’s Director of Jewish Life, Rebecca Joy Fletcher for a hands-on, Jewish take on outdoor mandala-making, in commemoration of the upcoming Jewish fast day of Tisha B'Av. Make your own unique mandala using organic materials from the Farm’s Food Forest and engage in a conversation about the many ways we can love -- freely and abundantly. No prior experience necessary and no need to identify as Jewish to attend and enjoy!
The Jewish fast day of Tisha B'av commemorates the destruction of the Temple, and many other sad moments in Jewish history. It is said that the Temple was destroyed because of baseless hatred, and that the best thing we can do as an antidote is to love for no reason as well. Think of it as abundant love.
Post mandala making light snacks and drinks.
$20/person. Ages 12 and up.

description:

Cost: $125 which includes all five classes (Limited number of full scholarships available. If a scholarship would help you come learn with us, please reach out directly to Rebecca Joy Fletcher:rebecca@coastalrootsfarm.org).
Let's dig into the spiritual revolution called Earth Based Judaism!
In this five-part series, we will gather in various locations on the Farm to explore five core values of Earth-Based Judaism and how to bring those values into your life.  You will ‘sow seeds’ to cultivate more resilience, earth-love, responsibility and joy.
Our learning will include sacred Jewish texts and wisdom, hearty conversation, on-our-feet exploration and play, quiet reflection, and creative writing. This series is open to adults of all ages, identities, abilities and experience levels. People of any or no faith background are always welcome. First-timers and returning students welcome!

title: Monthly Farm Tour
description:
Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm, a nonprofit community farm and education center rooted in Jewish values and regenerative agriculture. We grow over 80,000 pounds of organic produce each year on our 18-acre farm, with more than 75% donated to community members experiencing food insecurity. Each year, we also welcome over 15,000 youth and adults for outdoor education, Jewish life programs, and community events that inspire environmental stewardship, climate action, and connection to Jewish tradition.
On this guided tour, you’ll enjoy a gentle walk through our ever-evolving gardens, educational spaces, chicken coops, compost operations, and vibrant Food Forest. Learn about our unique growing methods and how we cultivate food, community, and justice.
The tour includes a hill and mixed terrain. We recommend wearing flat shoes, a sun hat, and bringing a water bottle. Learn about accessibility accommodations on the Farm.
Pre-registration required.Suggested donation: $20/per

title: Farm Film and Music Series – The Big Oyster & We Can Get There From Here Film Screening
description:
Join us this summer for a 5-part series featuring inspiring documentary films, local musicians, and meaningful conversations around equitable food systems, environmental justice, and caring for the planet.
We Can Get There From Here:  After years studying marine microplastics around the globe, Abby Barrows was ready to come home. In 2015, looking for a side-project that would keep her on the water, she bought the lease for an oyster farm in Deer Isle, Maine. When she saw the mountain of plastic gear that came with it, the side-project became something else.  Today, Deer Isle Oyster Co. is a flourishing family business, a proving ground for plastic-free mariculture gear, a new pier for a working waterfront reliant on one fishery, and a purveyor of some of the best oysters in the world. This is the story of one season on the farm, which also happens to be the story of an old island’s precarious present and potential future.

title: Plant. Prepare. Pray: Shavuot Learning and Celebration
description:
Shavuot, the Jewish holiday that commemorates the giving of the Torah on Mount Sinai, is also a deeply agricultural celebration. In ancient Israel, it marked the time when farmers brought bikkurim—the first fruits of their harvest—to Jerusalem as a gesture of gratitude and connection. After offering their choicest fruits and engaging in sacred reconnection, they returned home to enjoy the bounty of their labor.
Join Coastal Roots Farm and Temple Solel for Plant, Prepare, Pray—a joyful, soulful, and modern reimagining of bikkurim. Together, we’ll explore the Food Forest, engage in interactive learning, delve into sacred Jewish texts, and build meaningful connections with one another. We'll also enjoy traditional Shavuot treats like cheesecake and witness a stunning sunset over the Farm.


			
		
			
			Register Now!

title: Monthly Farm Tour
description:
Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm, a nonprofit community farm and education center rooted in Jewish values and regenerative agriculture. We grow over 80,000 pounds of organic produce each year on our 18-acre farm, with more than 75% donated to community members experiencing food insecurity. Each year, we also welcome over 15,000 youth and adults for outdoor education, Jewish life programs, and community events that inspire environmental stewardship, climate action, and connection to Jewish tradition.
On this guided tour, you’ll enjoy a gentle walk through our ever-evolving gardens, educational spaces, chicken coops, compost operations, and vibrant Food Forest. Learn about our unique growing methods and how we cultivate food, community, and justice.
The tour includes a hill and mixed terrain. We recommend wearing flat shoes, a sun hat, and bringing a water bottle. Learn about accessibility accommodations on the Farm.
Pre-registration required.Suggested donation: $20/per

title: The Relationships of Earth Based Judaism
description:
About the Series
Each of us can grow and deepen the ways we relate to our earth. Let's explore this through earth-based Judaism! This five-part journey offers a chance to explore powerful Jewish emotional-spiritual frameworks that deepen our relationship with the natural world. Meeting in beautiful spaces throughout the Farm, we’ll explore six core emotional-spiritual touchstones, such as awe, belonging, kinship, grief, and hope, that support our capacity to live with more awareness, responsibility, and resilience.
Our learning will blend sacred Jewish texts, interactive exploration, embodied practices, quiet reflection, creative writing, and spirited conversation. Participants are also invited to choose simple actions or rituals to try between sessions, cultivating connection both on and off the Farm.

title: Farm Film and Music Series – San Diego: America’s Wildest City Screening
description:
Experience Film, Music, and Meaningful Conversation on the Farm
Join us on the Farm for an evening of community, live music, and transformative storytelling. Part of our 5-part Farm Film & Music Series, this event invites guests to gather under the stars and engage in inspiring dialogue about regenerative agriculture, environmental justice, and the future of our food systems.
This screening will feature “San Diego: America’s Wildest City." San Diego is an oasis at the intersection of hostile worlds: the dividing line between the Mojave Desert to the East and the Pacific Ocean to the West; the Sierra Mountains to the North and Mexican border to the South. Meet the diverse array of wildlife thriving incongruously in a transformed landscape of manmade wilderness. From the dramatic rituals of grebes and the playful antics of ground squirrels to the thrilling hunts of orcas and the moonlit spawning of grunion, witness nature’s resilience and beauty amidst the urban sprawl.

title: Pride in the Sukkah
description:
LGBTQ+ Sukkot Celebration at Coastal Roots Farm
Come celebrate the beauty of LGBTQ+ community and the harvest holiday of Sukkot under Coastal Roots Farm’s big sukkah! Together we’ll gather with friends old and new, share festive food and drink, and explore the meaning of Sukkot through Earth Based Judaism learning.



		
	



	Sukkot is one of the most communal Jewish holidays. In the sukkah, a traditional temporary outdoor structure, we leave the walls open so that everyone feels welcome. Join us as we uplift each other, honor the harvest of the land, and celebrate the many harvests in our own lives.

Last Stand - A powerful look at efforts to protect a legacy forest in the Elwha River Watershed, highlighting the fight against deforestation and the role of grassroots activism in safeguarding vital ecosystems.

title: Earth Based Judaism: Qigong & Psalms
description:
Qigong & Psalms
Join Coastal Roots Farm and guest teacher Rabbi Susan Freeman, Director of Spiritual Life at Jewish Family Service, for an embodied exploration of Qigong & Psalms. In this four-part, interactive series, we will draw on qigong to experience the elements of water, wood, fire, earth, and metal in our bodies while connecting more deeply to the imagery and inspiration found in the ancient Book of Psalms.



		
	



	Through gentle movement and mindful breathing, qigong enhances physical health, mental clarity, and emotional well-being. The interconnectedness of all life is a theme woven throughout Psalms and we will let it inspire us as we connect more deeply with each other, Jewish tradition, and ourselves.
NOTE: No need to identify as Jewish to thoroughly enjoy. We welcome all to come and learn with us. Limited scholarships are available for anyone needing financial assistance.

title: Farm Film and Music Series – Faka’apa’apa & Out of Mined
description:
Experience Film, Music, and Meaningful Conversation on the Farm
Join us on the Farm for an evening of community, live music, and transformative storytelling. Part of our 5-part Farm Film & Music Series, this event invites guests to gather under the stars and engage in inspiring dialogue about regenerative agriculture, environmental justice, and the future of our food systems.
We're screening two documentaries: Faka'apa'apa and an early draft release of Out of Mined (coming March 2026).
Faka'apa'apa 
A documentary that follows Blue Latitudes Foundation researchers, Amber Sparks and Emily Hazelwood, as they work to understand the relationship between Tongan people and humpback whales, and the idea of Faka'apa'apa (respect). In Tonga, a Polynesian island nation, hunting humpbacks was legal and a main source of locals’ income until 1978, when the King banned all whaling within the Kingdom's waters. Since then, locals have made a shift toward ecotourism through whale watching, and whale pop

title: Jewish Studio Project
description:
Jewish Studio Project at Coastal Roots Farm
Join us for a guided experience with the Jewish Studio Process—an approach that combines Jewish learning, creative exploration, and personal reflection. Through text, conversation, and hands-on materials, we’ll open to a deeper encounter with beauty: not just how things appear, but how they move us, stir us, call us closer to what matters.
No artistic experience is needed—just a spirit of curiosity and a willingness to play.
Facilitated by Suzanne Bates

title: Earth Based Judaism: Qigong & the Poetry of Psalms
description:
Qigong & the Poetry of Psalms
Following the success of a fall class, guest teacher Rabbi Susan Freeman returns to the Farm for a joyful, cross-cultural exploration of Qigong & the Poetry of Tehillim— Psalms in Hebrew.
In this six-part series, we will bring together two rich traditions in order to enhance physical and spiritual health. With its gentle movements and guided breathing, Qigong will help us to experience the elements of water, wood, fire, earth, and metal in our bodies. Tehillim will connect us more deeply with our hearts.



		
	



	Credited in part to King David, Tehillim are a foundational meditative and personal prayer practice within Jewish tradition. In addition to appearing throughout the prayerbook, they can be recited privately at moments of need (for someone ill, for a better world, for a personal struggle.) Their poetry is beautiful and rich with nature and the interconnectedness of all life!
 



	 
Event Details
📅 Dates: Sundays, January 18, 25, February 1, 8,

title: Intro to Earth-Based Judaism Series (4 classes)
description:
In this four-part pilot class, we will gather in lovely locations across the Farm to explore 4 basic pathways into the spiritual revolution called Earth-Based Judaism -- and how to bring those pathways back into your life. The goal is more resilience, earth-love, responsibility, and joy.
Our learning will include sacred Jewish texts and wisdom, hearty conversation, on-our-feet exploration and play, and moments of quiet reflection. We'll also provide coffee, pastries, and time to schmooze.
The classes will be nourished by the connections we forge with each other as much as by the content we explore, and that’s why we are capping registration at 30. This series is open to adults of all ages, identities, abilities, and experience levels. You do not need to identify as Jewish to attend!
$100/person for whole series (4 classes). We strongly encourage registrants to attend all 4 classes. We are unable to prorate for partial attendance or offer refunds. Max capacity 30 people.

title: Compassionate Communication Webinar Series
description: This session explores how the University of Kentucky College of Medicine Office for Organizational Well-Being (OWB) listens deeply to frontline clinicians and transforms that feedback into meaningful, multi-level culture change. Through its Department Enhancement Project (DEP) framework and the Organizational Well-Being Executive Leadership Team (OWBELT) Action Plan, OWB has created intentional structures that surface root issues, elevate frontline perspectives, and translate insights into coordinated department- and system-level improvements. Participants will learn how these interconnected frameworks foster transparency, collaboration, and accountability, and how to adapt similar ground-up approaches to create strategic interventions and accelerate culture change within their own organizations.

title: Course Location
description: Our class location in Anaheim at the Holiday Inn Anaheim Area Resort is easily accessible off of the 5 freeway and offers plenty of free convenient parking. The location is just minutes away from Downtown Disney and is convenient to nearby restaurants, shops, and entertainment highlights.

title: Course Location
description: As a host site organization for the UC San Diego OTIEC, the Kern Community College District (KCCD) proudly offers OSHA numbered courses and other industry specific safety classes. Serving Bakersfield and Kern counties, the KCCD classrooms are conveniently located in downtown Bakersfield. Additionally, KCCD trains individuals for careers supporting both the clean energy and traditional oil and gas industries.

title: Course Location
description: As a host site location for the UC San Diego OSHA Training Institute Education Center, the Guam Contractor’s Association (GCA) proudly offers OSHA-authorized training. The GCA has provided a wide variety of safety conferences and resources to the island since 1990. They are a non-profit corporation dedicated to promoting the common business interests of its member-contractors, subcontractors, material suppliers, equipment leasers and other businesses related to the construction industry in Guam

title: Course Location
description: As a host site organization for the UC San Diego OSHA Training Institute Education Center, the Nevada Contractors Association (Associated General Contractors – Las Vegas) helps to provide OSHA’s premier education network courses. The classroom location is easily accessible and is just 15 minutes away from the bustling Las Vegas Strip.

title: Course Locations
description: As a host site for the UC San Diego OTIEC, the University of California Los Angeles and University of California Irvine Southern California NIOSH Education and Research Center (SCERC) offers OSHA training in two convenient Los Angeles locations. The first location is located on the UCLA campus at the UCLA School of Public Health. All other courses are held at the historic Van de Kamp Center.

title: Course Location
description: As a host site for the UC San Diego OTIEC, the Nevada Chapter Associated General Contractors (Nevada Chapter - AGC) provides essential training to assist members with expanding their knowledge on workforce development, safety and supervisory training. Their mission is to support their members, enhance the image and awareness of the construction industry and the quality of life through skill, integrity and responsibility.

title: Course Location
description: As the home base for the UC San Diego OTIEC, San Diego offers students the ultimate destination location to receive all of their OSHA training. Many students bring their families in order to take advantage of the many exciting attractions, such as miles of beautiful beaches, the famous San Diego Zoo, USS Midway Museum, Balboa Park and Legoland.

title: Course Location
description: As a host site for the UC San Diego OTIEC, UC Santa Barbara’s Professional and Continuing Education (PaCE) helps to offer high quality, standards-based OSHA training for Santa Barbara, Ventura and San Luis Obispo Counties.

title: Course Location
description: For nearly 20 years, The American Society of Safety Professionals (ASSP) Chapter in Tucson has proudly offered authorized OSHA Training courses, as a host site for UC San Diego’s OSHA Training Institute Education Center.

title: Course Location
description: UC San Diego’s OTIEC is proud to offer training at the Construction Training Center of the Pacific. The CTC is a one-stop comprehensive training and educational resource center established by the Building Industry Association of Hawai‘i as its workforce development arm. The CTC provides academic and vocational training, career counseling, job awareness, and related supportive services that address critical labor shortages in the rapidly expanding construction industry.

title: Leader Programs
description:
Our Leaders Programs engage young people along a pathway to college and career to ensure students who want to become science and conservation leaders are provided the tools to get there.
These programs engage over 100 middle school, high school, and college-age young people per year in a series of interconnected after-school and summer programs and support services. Leaders offers a pathway for underrepresented students to progress from secondary school through university and on to careers in science and conservation.
We prepare students – our Ocean Leaders – through a unique approach that engages students in authentic science experiences which build on their talents, passions, and intellect; sets high expectations; and use mentoring and coaching. This approach empowers students and provides the opportunity to build the skills needed to overcome challenges on their own.

Scientific research
Engaging students in authentic scientific research ha

title: Student Programs
description:
Student Programs engage students and their teachers within their instructional settings to ensure every student is engaged in hands-on science.
Ocean Science Explorers
The cornerstone of our student programs, Ocean Science Explorers provides early, continued exposure to science by facilitating science learning in the classroom and field and professional development and resources for classroom teachers. Students participate in Ocean Science Explorers over four years, as the program is provided from the third through sixth grades. The program design uses ocean science and local resources as a platform to teach science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM). Students learn what science is, how science works, the essential principles of science, and how science is relevant to their lives. All curricula are grade level specific and aligned with Next Generation Science Standards.
Program staff visit each class six times to implement three core components: hands-on experiential learning in

title: Community Programs
description:
Community Programs engage young people, their families, and neighbors within the school-shed to ensure everyone sees science and conservation as relevant to their lives.
Watershed Avengers
Watershed Avengers is the cornerstone of these programs. It is designed to use ocean science, watershed education, and local resources such as canyons, as a platform to teach science and engage participants in environmental stewardship actions. Through participation, young people, their families, and neighbors will learn how the world works and how they can make a difference, while actively transforming the canyons into safe and healthy environments.
Watershed Avengers takes a unique, comprehensive approach that is implemented through:

Youth-led canyon restoration events
A student internship
A cross-sector community alliance

This comprehensive approach is used to create a productive connection between community member’s neighborhood canyons.

title: The Party in the Park | San Diego Automotive Museum Event
description:
SAVE THE DATESeptember 26th at 5pmSAN DIEGO AUTOMOTIVE MUSEUMJOIN THE PRIORITY REGISTRATION LISTJoin us for an unforgettable evening ofglamour, jazz, and meaningful impactin true Roaring 20s style—where Art Deco elegance and live jazz set the stage for a dazzling night in support of the San Diego Automotive Museum.A 1920s ROARING AUTOMOTIVE AFFAIR
Step into the glamour of the Roaring 20s, where the San Diego Automotive Museum comes alive with Art Deco elegance and the thrill of a bygone era. Guests will explore a stunning exhibit of 1920s automobiles, mingle in richly decorated spaces, enjoy crafted cocktails, and savor elevated hors d'oeuvres—all while soaking in the music, style, and energy that defined a generation. It’s an evening designed to delight the senses, spark conversation, and celebrate both the history of automotive innovation and the future it inspires.A 1920s ROARING AUTOMOTIVE AFFAIR

title: Dr. Perry Mansfield
description:
HONORINGDr. Perry MansfieldHIS MISSION
To inspire hope, expand the health and well-being of each patient with head and neck/skull base disease, by providing personalized optimal care through integrated clinical practice, and research.About Perry Mansfield MD, FRCSCChairman and Founder, The Mansfield CompaniesWe are truly honored to announce that Perry Mansfield, MD will be the honoree at this year’s THE Party in the Park—an evening dedicated to celebrating a man whose heart, generosity, and passion have left an indelible mark on our community.

For many in San Diego, Dr. Mansfield is first known as a compassionate and trusted physician. Throughout his distinguished medical career, he has cared for patients and families with empathy, integrity, and a steadfast commitment to excellence. His work reflects not only professional achievement, but a genuine desire to serve and uplift others.

Those who know him well also know that his love of automobiles runs just as deep. Dr.

title: Ignite 2025
description:
SEPTEMBER 2025The Party in the Park
. . . IGNITE!Party In The Park An Evening at the Grand Prix
The San Diego Automotive Museum’s sold-out “Party in the Park” transformed Balboa Park into a night of elegance, excitement, and celebration. Inspired by the thrill of the Grand Prix, more than 400 guests came together to raise over $850,000 in support of the Museum’s growth and education initiatives.An Evening at the Grand Prix
The San Diego Automotive Museum’s sold-out “Party in the Park” transformed Balboa Park into a night of elegance, excitement, and celebration. title: The Ray Brock Hall of Fame | San Diego Automotive Museum
description:
Venue Rentals  >  The Hall of FameThe Ray Brock Hall of Fame
Elevate your next event—literally and figuratively—in our stunning Mezzanine. Overlooking the main exhibition floor, this airy, light-filled space offers a unique vantage point that immerses guests in the museum’s atmosphere while providing a sense of exclusivity and privacy.
With its open layout the mezzanine is ideal for receptions, cocktail gatherings, corporate events, intimate dinners, and special celebrations. Guests can mingle against a backdrop of artworks and exhibits below, enjoying a setting that blends sophistication, culture, and inspiration.
The space includes flexible seating and staging options, museum-quality lighting, and dedicated event support. Whether you’re planning an elegant evening event or a contemporary daytime gathering, the mezzanine provides a memorable setting that distinguishes your occasion from the ordinary.
Host your next event on a level all its own.

title: The Grove | San Diego Automotive Museum
description:
Venue Rentals  >  The GroveThe Grove
The museum’s Grove offers a welcoming, scenic location for gatherings that feel relaxed yet special. Whether you’re planning a garden-style reception, a family celebration, or a community event, the space provides room to spread out, enjoy the fresh air, and connect in a setting inspired by art and nature.
With flexible arrangements and supportive staff, it’s the perfect place to bring people together. With the Cabrillo Bridge and view of Downtown San Diego, the Museum's Grove is sure to please.
The rental fee includes:

Dedicated museum staff
City permit (must be filed a minimum of 60 days prior)
Admission to the Museum.

A list of preferred vendors for rentals is available. REQUEST INFO NOW

title: The Exhibit Floor | San Diego Automotive Museum
description:
Venue Rentals  >  The Exhibit FloorThe Exhibit Floor
Step into a setting where art, technology, and history come together to create an unforgettable backdrop for your next event. Our Museum Exhibit Space offers a distinctive venue that blends architectural elegance with curated displays, providing guests with a one-of-a-kind experience the moment they arrive.
Designed to inspire conversation and spark imagination, this flexible gallery space accommodates a wide range of private events—including corporate receptions, intimate celebrations, lectures, fundraisers, and cocktail gatherings. Surrounded by thoughtfully arranged exhibits, guests can enjoy an immersive atmosphere that elevates any occasion.
Amenities include professional event support, museum-quality lighting, sound system and wi-fi. Whether you're hosting a sophisticated evening soirée or a daytime program with cultural flair, the Museum Exhibit Space offers a memorable setting that stands apart from traditional venues.

title: The Classroom or Boardroom | San Diego Automotive Museum
description:
Venue Rentals  >  The Classroom or BoardroomThe Classroom or Boardroom
Designed for productivity, collaboration, and comfort, our Classroom or Boardroom spaces offer flexible environments ideal for meetings, trainings, workshops, and small-group gatherings. Located within the museum, these rooms combine modern functionality with the inspiring atmosphere of a cultural setting.
The Boardroom features a polished, executive-style layout complete with a large conference table, ergonomic seating, three built in smart TVs, video conference ready, Keurig and cold beverages, perfect for leadership meetings, planning sessions, and private discussions. Your rental fee includes admission to the Museum.
The Classroom offers an adaptable setup suited for lectures, hands-on workshops, seminars, and educational programs. title: Meditation & Mindfulness
description:
Discover simple and effective ways to reduce stress, bring more balance and happiness into your life through the practices of meditation and mindfulness.
Learn easy ways to integrate these techniques into your wellness plan.
Refresh or Reboot your meditation practice.
Experience first-hand the research-based benefits of these techniques:
Improved Memory * Reduced Inflammation * Improved Mood
Reduced Anxiety * Increased Creativity and Productivity * Reduced Food Cravings
Class features: Sound Meditation, Guided Body Scan, Gratitude Exercise, Mantra Meditation, Breathing, Aromatherapy and Mindfulness Eating Exercise
Instructor: Abby Murphy
Certified Meditation Teacher through Chopra Center for Well-Being
Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction Certification by Dr. Jon Kabat-Zinn, UMass Medical Center

*Space is limited to 20 participants, so arrive early, it is first come, first serve.

Our ukulele instructor will guide students through:
How to hold, tune, and strum the ukuleleChords, rhythms, and beginner songsPlaying together as a groupBuilding on skills for returning studentsFun musical games and activities
Show off your skills at the end with a group performance!

Sponsored by the Friends of the Fallbrook Library and Fallbrook Music Society.

title: Silent Study & Work Area
description:
The Libraryâs North Community Room will be set up with tables and chairs for silent work, study, or other quiet tasks on Mondays (11:00 a.m.-5:00 p.m.) & Fridays (11:00 a.m.-4:00 p.m.). A limited number of power outlets for charging will be available. The library does not have charging cables for specific devices.
Guidelines:
No video or phone calls. Bring headphones to listen to audio.All devices/technology set to silent or vibrate. No food. Covered drinks only. Area available to teens (grades 9-12) & adults.
Please be respectful of others. If the task you are completing will make noise or is disturbing others, you may be asked to leave the silent area.The Silent Study & Work Area program will be on break between April 23, 2026 through June 10, 2026, due to Community Room unavailability.

description:
Children in grades K-5 are invited to join us at the library every Wednesday for a different Afterschool Adventure! Supplies are guaranteed for the first 30 participants.
April 1st: Gumdrop structures: Build a structure using toothpicks and gumdrops. At the end of the event, we will award prizes for the tallest structure, and the structure which can hold the most weight!
April 8th: BINGO: Join us for a fun game of BINGO! 
April 15th: Coloring: Help us color large spring mandala coloring posters!
April 22nd: Paper Airplanes: Design a paper airplane, then compete to see whose can fly the farthest! A prize will be awarded to the winning airplane!April 29th: Build a catapult: Create your own catapult, or follow our easy instructions to design one. Whichever catapult can fling the farthest will receive a prize!

title: Silent Study & Work Area
description:
The Libraryâs North Community Room will be set up with tables and chairs for silent work, study, or other quiet tasks on Mondays (11:00 a.m.-5:00 p.m.) & Fridays (11:00 a.m.-4:00 p.m.). A limited number of power outlets for charging will be available. The library does not have charging cables for specific devices.
Guidelines:
No video or phone calls. Bring headphones to listen to audio.All devices/technology set to silent or vibrate. No food. Covered drinks only. Area available to teens (grades 9-12) & adults.
Please be respectful of others. If the task you are completing will make noise or is disturbing others, you may be asked to leave the silent area.The Silent Study & Work Area program will be on break between April 23, 2026 through June 10, 2026, due to Community Room unavailability.

title: Meditation & Mindfulness
description:
Discover simple and effective ways to reduce stress, bring more balance and happiness into your life through the practices of meditation and mindfulness.
Learn easy ways to integrate these techniques into your wellness plan.
Refresh or Reboot your meditation practice.
Experience first-hand the research-based benefits of these techniques:
Improved Memory * Reduced Inflammation * Improved Mood
Reduced Anxiety * Increased Creativity and Productivity * Reduced Food Cravings
Class features: Sound Meditation, Guided Body Scan, Gratitude Exercise, Mantra Meditation, Breathing, Aromatherapy and Mindfulness Eating Exercise
Instructor: Abby Murphy
Certified Meditation Teacher through Chopra Center for Well-Being
Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction Certification by Dr. Jon Kabat-Zinn, UMass Medical Center

*Space is limited to 20 participants, so arrive early, it is first come, first serve.

title: CSA San Diego - Fair Housing Table Information Table
description: CSA San Diego County is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization whose primary mission is to promote social justice and public welfare through programs, services, and advocacy against all forms of discrimination, including advocacy for the eradication of housing discrimination to assure equal housing opportunities for all. CSA can assist with: Discrimination in Housing, Illegal Rent Increases, Evictions Notices, Illegal Evictions, Fair Housing Counseling, Tenant-Landlord Mediation, First Time Homebuyer Education, and Fair Housing workshops where tenants and landlords can learn about their rights.

title: Meditation & Mindfulness
description:
Discover simple and effective ways to reduce stress, bring more balance and happiness into your life through the practices of meditation and mindfulness.
Learn easy ways to integrate these techniques into your wellness plan.
Refresh or Reboot your meditation practice.
Experience first-hand the research-based benefits of these techniques:
Improved Memory * Reduced Inflammation * Improved Mood
Reduced Anxiety * Increased Creativity and Productivity * Reduced Food Cravings
Class features: Sound Meditation, Guided Body Scan, Gratitude Exercise, Mantra Meditation, Breathing, Aromatherapy and Mindfulness Eating Exercise
Instructor: Abby Murphy
Certified Meditation Teacher through Chopra Center for Well-Being
Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction Certification by Dr. Jon Kabat-Zinn, UMass Medical Center

*Space is limited to 20 participants, so arrive early, it is first come, first serve.

title: Meditation & Mindfulness
description:
Discover simple and effective ways to reduce stress, bring more balance and happiness into your life through the practices of meditation and mindfulness.
Learn easy ways to integrate these techniques into your wellness plan.
Refresh or Reboot your meditation practice.
Experience first-hand the research-based benefits of these techniques:
Improved Memory * Reduced Inflammation * Improved Mood
Reduced Anxiety * Increased Creativity and Productivity * Reduced Food Cravings
Class features: Sound Meditation, Guided Body Scan, Gratitude Exercise, Mantra Meditation, Breathing, Aromatherapy and Mindfulness Eating Exercise
Instructor: Abby Murphy
Certified Meditation Teacher through Chopra Center for Well-Being
Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction Certification by Dr. Jon Kabat-Zinn, UMass Medical Center

*Space is limited to 20 participants, so arrive early, it is first come, first serve.

title: "To Climb a Gold Mountain" Movie Screening
description:
Celebrate Asian American Pacific Islander Month with a community screening of the acclaimed documentary, "To Climb a Gold Mountain" and meet the filmmaker, Dr. Alex M. Azmi, who will answer questions following the film.
The film is about the immigrant experience, as told through the inspirational and poignant stories of four women of Asian descent who lived in America from the 1850s to the present day. One interview is with the bestselling author, Lisa See.There will be a table hosted by the Employee Resource Group (ERG), APACE (Asian Pacific Alliance of County Employees).
* Light refreshments will be provided.

title: Hatha Yoga
description:
Join us Mondays and Wednesdays at 6:00 pm with instructor Rebecca. Bring your own mat and water.
Hatha Yoga originated in Indian in the 15th Century. This type of Yoga is a slow-paced, gentle, and focused on breathing, and connecting the mind and body. Vinyasa Yoga-Much like Hatha, Vinyasa covers basic poses and breath synchronized movement. This variety of Hatha Yoga uses sun salutations, and other asanas to help keep you moving comfortably and safely in your body.

Discover simple and effective ways to reduce stress, bring more balance and happiness into your life through the practices of meditation and mindfulness.
Learn easy ways to integrate these techniques into your wellness plan.
Refresh or Reboot your meditation practice.
Experience first-hand the research-based benefits of these techniques:
Improved Memory * Reduced Inflammation * Improved Mood
Reduced Anxiety * Increased Creativity and Productivity * Reduced Food Cravings
Class features: Sound Meditation, Guided Body Scan, Gratitude Exercise, Mantra Meditation, Breathing, Aromatherapy and Mindfulness Eating Exercise
Instructor: Abby Murphy
Certified Meditation Teacher through Chopra Center for Well-Being
Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction Certification by Dr. Jon Kabat-Zinn, UMass Medical Center

*Space is limited to 20 participants, so arrive early, it is first come, first serve.

title: Ukulele Learning Lab
description:
Join us for a fun, hands-on 6 week Ukulele course designed for both new and returning students! Each one-hour session blends hands-on playing, musical discovery, and creative expression. No experience needed! Ukuleles will be provided, unless you would like to bring your own. For kids ages 7-12.
Our ukulele instructor will guide students through:
How to hold, tune, and strum the ukuleleChords, rhythms, and beginner songsPlaying together as a groupBuilding on skills for returning studentsFun musical games and activities
Show off your skills at the end with a group performance!
*Pre-registration is required. Spaces are limited. For registration, please visit the Ask Desk. Parents/caregivers are required to be present during classes.
Sponsored by the Friends of the Fallbrook Library and Fallbrook Music Society.

title: Silent Study & Work Area
description:
The Libraryâs North Community Room will be set up with tables and chairs for silent work, study, or other quiet tasks on Mondays (11:00 a.m.-5:00 p.m.) & Fridays (11:00 a.m.-4:00 p.m.). A limited number of power outlets for charging will be available. The library does not have charging cables for specific devices.
Guidelines:
No video or phone calls. Bring headphones to listen to audio.All devices/technology set to silent or vibrate. No food. Covered drinks only. Area available to teens (grades 9-12) & adults.
Please be respectful of others. If the task you are completing will make noise or is disturbing others, you may be asked to leave the silent area.The Silent Study & Work Area program will be on break between April 23, 2026 through June 10, 2026, due to Community Room unavailability.

title: Imperial Beach Community Clinic Information Table
description:
Imperial Beach Community Clinic (IBCC) works to promote a healthy community by providing quality health care and resources, with a focus on the underserved. Imperial Beach Community Clinic is a family-friendly health care organization serving San Diego's South Bay since 1971.
This information table provides the community with general information about IBCC services.
https://www.ibclinic.org/

At the end of the event, we will award prizes for the tallest structure, and the structure which can hold the most weight!
April 8th: BINGO: Join us for a fun game of BINGO! 
April 15th: Coloring: Help us color large spring mandala coloring posters!
April 22nd: Paper Airplanes: Design a paper airplane, then compete to see whose can fly the farthest! A prize will be awarded to the winning airplane!April 29th: Build a catapult: Create your own catapult, or follow our easy instructions to design one. Whichever catapult can fling the farthest will receive a prize!

title: Meditation & Mindfulness
description:
Discover simple and effective ways to reduce stress, bring more balance and happiness into your life through the practices of meditation and mindfulness.
Learn easy ways to integrate these techniques into your wellness plan.
Refresh or Reboot your meditation practice.
Experience first-hand the research-based benefits of these techniques:
Improved Memory * Reduced Inflammation * Improved Mood
Reduced Anxiety * Increased Creativity and Productivity * Reduced Food Cravings
Class features: Sound Meditation, Guided Body Scan, Gratitude Exercise, Mantra Meditation, Breathing, Aromatherapy and Mindfulness Eating Exercise
Instructor: Abby Murphy
Certified Meditation Teacher through Chopra Center for Well-Being
Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction Certification by Dr. Jon Kabat-Zinn, UMass Medical Center

*Space is limited to 20 participants, so arrive early, it is first come, first serve.

title: Coffee and Conversation: RUTH Remember Us The Holocaust: Sandra Scheller, Holocaust Educator
description: Sandra âSandyâ Scheller is an American Holocaust educator, curator, and community advocate based in San Diego, California. She is the founder and curator of âRUTH: Remember Us The Holocaust,â a traveling educational exhibit dedicated to preserving the stories of Holocaust survivors who settled in San Diego County. Schellerâs work focuses on ensuring that the voices of Holocaust survivors continue to be heard as their generation diminishes. Through exhibits, public speaking, and educational outreach, she advocates for the creation of a permanent Holocaust museum in San Diego and the preservation of survivor testimonies for future generations.

title: San Diego History from 1915-Today: The Forces That Shaped Our City
description:
Celebrate the 250th History of the United StatesSan Diego from 1915-Today: The Forces That Shaped Our City
This one-hour presentation explores San Diegoâs history from 1915 to today, beginning with the Panama-California Exposition and the creation of Balboa Park and the San Diego Zoo. From the challenges of the Great Depression to the tremendous growth during and after World War I and World War II, and into the rise of tourism and the 21st century, discover the key forces that have shaped San Diego into the city it is today.

title: Meditation & Mindfulness
description:
Discover simple and effective ways to reduce stress, bring more balance and happiness into your life through the practices of meditation and mindfulness.
Learn easy ways to integrate these techniques into your wellness plan.
Refresh or Reboot your meditation practice.
Experience first-hand the research-based benefits of these techniques:
Improved Memory * Reduced Inflammation * Improved Mood
Reduced Anxiety * Increased Creativity and Productivity * Reduced Food Cravings
Class features: Sound Meditation, Guided Body Scan, Gratitude Exercise, Mantra Meditation, Breathing, Aromatherapy and Mindfulness Eating Exercise
Instructor: Abby Murphy
Certified Meditation Teacher through Chopra Center for Well-Being
Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction Certification by Dr. Jon Kabat-Zinn, UMass Medical Center

*Space is limited to 20 participants, so arrive early, it is first come, first serve.

title: Mountain, Sea and Desert Birding Tour
description: Join us for the return of our Mountain, Sea and Desert Birding Tour! Two expert birders, Gjon Hazard and Thomas Myers, will lead the group on a three-day birding class from the mountains to the (Salton) sea to the desert. Gjon and Thomas have scouted the best birding sites of the region and are eager to help you get your best bird sightings of the year. They’ll also discuss the ecology, conservation, and taxonomy of the birds that we’ll see.

title: Adventure Hike: Mortero Palms to Goat Canyon Trestle
description: Join ABF for our first intermediate hike of the season! This scenic and moderately challenging six-mile hike will gain approximately 1000 ft of elevation and take you through some of the most fascinating landscapes in ABDSP. Participants will hike The Slot, a narrow and winding sandstone canyon known for its dramatic, towering walls and tight passages that make for a thrilling adventure.

title: Interpretive Hike: Anza-Borrego’s Active Faults
description: Join us for a hands-on exploration of the Coyote Creek strand of the San Jacinto Fault, where the landscape itself reveals the movement of Earth’s crust. This guided field trip highlights classic examples of fault geomorphology and offers an up-close look at the evidence scientists use to map and interpret active faults.

title: Mountain, Sea and Desert Birding Tour
description: Join us for the return of our Mountain, Sea and Desert Birding Tour! Two expert birders, Gjon Hazard and Thomas Myers, will lead the group on a three-day birding class from the mountains to the (Salton) sea to the desert. Gjon and Thomas have scouted the best birding sites of the region and are eager to help you get your best bird sightings of the year. They’ll also discuss the ecology, conservation, and taxonomy of the birds that we’ll see.

title: Astronomy Workshop: “The Christmas Star”
description: Do you ever wonder what the stars looked like thousands of years ago or what may have been the “Christmas star?” In this astronomy presentation, you’ll explore an age-old mystery of what may have been the “Star” seen at the time of the birth of Christ. We’ll look at the science, history, folklore, and mythology related to this event in an attempt to understand what the “Star” may have been, who actually saw it and the importance of the date December 25th.

title: AEF Little Learners
description:
Learn & Play
Little Learners
			
			
				
				
				
				
			
				
				
			
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
					
					
						Educational FUN
						
					
				
			
				
				
				
				
				


 AEF Little Learners
AEF Little Learners is a fun interactive story hour for toddlers and preschoolers held throughout the year and consists of age-appropriate picture books, songs, and includes playtime and crafts. Children also get a soft introduction into our “Wheel of Experts” which is a multiple pathway program that brings STEAM based learning to the classroom. AEF Little Learners is held the 2nd Monday of every month at Bethel Christian Assembly 
We hope to see you there! 



Address: 1929 Arnold Way, Alpine, CA 91901

Wheel of Experts
			
			
				
				
				
				
			
				
				
			
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
					
					
						Educational FUN
						
					
				
			
				
				
				
				
				 STEAM Based Learning
“Wheel of Experts” is a multiple pathway program that brings STEAM based learning to the classroom. Each week, students from Kindergarten-8th grade have the opportunity to participate in hands on enrichment topics such as Coding/Robotics, Science Labs, Music, and Gardening/Aquaponics – creating a direct pathway to electives offered in 6th to 8th grade. Instruction is to Next Generation Science Standards.
Please help defray the costs of this unique, continuing educational event  by partnering with us or with your donation at this link:

title: Punkin' Chunkin'
description:
Learn & Play
Punkin’ Chunkin’
			
			
				
				
				
				
			
				
				
			
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
					
					
						Educational FUN
						
					
				
			
				
				
				
				
				
					
					
						What is Punkin’ Chunkin’?
						Using their knowledge of history, math, and physics, students build trebuchets, a medieval siege engine, to launch pumpkins and golf balls, competing for distance and accuracy. They practice teamwork while learning wood handling and the use of hand tools. It is also a popular activity around the world. The European Championship is held annually in Belgium, followed by the World Championship in Delaware the first full weekend after Halloween.
					
				
			
				
				
				
				
				DONATE

title: Camps
description:
CALENDAR
ReadyBreak Camps
			
			
				
				
				
				
			
				
				
			
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				ELEMENTARY SCHOOL CAMPS
Ready Break Camps 2026
This year, we are excited to introduce a new format for our Elementary School Summer Camps! Instead of selecting a single subject for the week, campers will register by incoming grade level and rotate through a variety of fun activities throughout the week.
Each day, students will experience a different subject area such as art, cooking, STEM, PE, games, and more. Campers will stay with the same group for the entire week, allowing them to enjoy a wide range of activities.
During registration, parents will also have the option to request one buddy and we will do our best to place friends in the same group.
For more info on our Middle School camps – click here

title: Camps FAQ
description:
Information
Frequently Asked Questions
			
			
				
				
				
				
			
				
				
			
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				Got A Question? Get your answers here.
			
			
				
				
				
				
			
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				
				I have registered and paid, now what?
				You will receive a confirmation email with your registration selection, once it has been processed. Parents/Guardians will also receive a welcome letter from their student’s instructor approx. one week prior to their camp assignment with information specific to the camp.
			
				
				
				
				
				Will meals be provided for my child?
				No meal service will be provided. Students are encouraged to bring a snack for the recess period/s and a water bottle. There will be no working water fountains, but available water stations for refills. Students participating in a full day program should bring a sack lunch.

title: THE ECOLOGIK INSTITUTE — Junior Scientist Day 2024 at Cabrillo National Monument
description: Calling all Junior Scientists! On this National Public Lands Day, join an epic quest to learn about the science and engineering that make your national parks so special from real scientists and engineers! Earn a special Junior Scientist Badge as your reward.

title: THE ECOLOGIK INSTITUTE — Biologgers with EcoLogik
description: Recommended age: 9-15. How can computer programming help with wildlife conservation? Conduct an experiment with technology that helps scientists understand how temperature changes can affect animal health. Registration Required.

title: THE ECOLOGIK INSTITUTE — Biodiversity Snapshot with EcoLogik
description: Recommended age: 9-15. Scientists can’t be everywhere at once, so it’s important to crowdsource science! Learn how you can contribute to scientific discoveries using tools many of us have in the palm of our hands. Registration Required.

title: THE ECOLOGIK INSTITUTE — Camera Traps with EcoLogik
description: Recommended age 9-15. How do scientists monitor the park when they aren’t around? Explore technology that enables scientists to learn about the ecosystem and try your hand at using this tech yourself. Registration Required.

title: THE ECOLOGIK INSTITUTE — 3D PRINTING WITH ECOLOGIK
description: Ever wanted to learn how to 3D print? Discover how to create a model of some of the animals we find in the Cabrillo tidepools. Registration Required. Recommended age 9-15.

title: THE ECOLOGIK INSTITUTE — RADIO TELEMETRY WITH ECOLOGIK
description:
What is Radio Telemetry? Learn how scientists track animals in the wild through radio signals, and what that data can tell us about an ecosystem by mapping your findings.

Recommended for ages 9-15.

title: EcoVivarium Reptile Sanctuary and Museum
description:
Experience the Wonder Together

At EcoVivarium, our events bring learning, connection, and adventure to life. From hands-on animal encounters and seasonal celebrations to community fundraisers, workshops, and volunteer gatherings — each event is designed to inspire curiosity and build meaningful connections between people and the natural world.
Whether you’re joining us for a family day, a special behind-the-scenes program, or a community outreach experience, you’ll discover opportunities to explore, learn, and support our mission in exciting and memorable ways.
Check out our upcoming events and be part of the EcoVivarium journey!

title: Workshops
description:
Our Ocean-Based STEAM Workshops highlight the NMMF’s discoveries about marine mammals to promote creative thinking and inspire a greater commitment to the conservation of marine mammals and their habitats. Our program offers a unique opportunity for you to:




Connect with Marine Mammal Experts: Our Community Engagement staff and volunteers include marine mammal scientists, veterinarians, conservationists, and care experts. Learn from their experiences, insights, and ongoing field work.



Participate in Interactive Workshops: Join us in hands-on workshops, where you can immerse yourself in the world of marine mammal science. From understanding acoustic communication to marine mammal health assessments, you’ll gain valuable knowledge and skills.



Discover Marine Mammal Conservation: Learn about the critical efforts and methods used to protect these incredible creatures and their habitats in a dynamic learning environment






Educational Workshops (Grades K-12)



As part of our un

title: You Can Be a STEM Professional
description:
You Can Be a STEM Professional Workshop



Inspiring the Next Generation 





Participants will hear from STEM (science, technology, engineering, and mathematics) professionals in a wide variety of fields, including marine mammal science and veterinary medicine, while participating in hands-on STEM demonstrations.



Our STEM professionals will briefly discuss their career path, why they are passionate about their work, encourage students to continue on an academic path towards STEM careers, and show ways families can continue to support their kids! 











You Can Be a STEM Professional – Workshop Resources









You Can Be a STEM Professional – Worksheet

title: Pop for a Porpoise
description:
POP for a Porpoise Workshop



Prevent Ocean Plastics



In this workshop, students will focus on ways that plastic pollution affects marine animals, such as entanglement and ingestion of plastics by dolphins, whales, sea turtles, sea birds, and fish. We specifically highlight our efforts to save the Vaquita porpoise, the most endangered marine mammal in the world (www.VaquitaCPR.org). 



Our hands-on activities focus on the plastic lifecycle, how plastic affects sea life & climate, a watershed model, and recycling. We emphasize that small efforts can collectively make a big difference. We hope that students who complete the POP for a Porpoise program will have the knowledge and drive to make our earth a cleaner, healthier place, and that they will be excited to educate and inspire others.

title: Dolphin Doctor Workshop
description:
In this workshop, students are guided through an interactive mock dolphin health assessment, explore the field of marine mammal bioacoustics, and learn how scientists can use sound to help care for marine mammals. From conducting health assessments with trained dolphins to working alongside wild pods, our team is dedicated to marine mammal care. Learn from our experts at our exciting workshops!















Let’s Dive in! Discover the Online Doctor Dolphin Workshop









Take the Dolphin Doctor Challenge!





NEW! The NMMF now offers a web-based introductory course on marine mammal veterinary medicine for students of all ages. Like the in-person Dolphin Doctor workshop, the virtual course includes information on:




Dolphin and sea lion anatomy, physiology, and adaptations to the marine environment



Veterinary health exam process



Conservation issues that affect wild marine mammals around the world




Each lesson includes a video, written course material, and other optional

title: Voices in the Sea
description:
Voices in the Sea Workshop



Marine mammals rely on sound for communication, foraging, navigation, and predator avoidance. The NMMF explores the sounds these animals make, the sounds they hear, and the effects of human-made noise on their behavior and physiology.



The NMMF has developed advanced instrumentation to test the hearing of wild marine mammals and determine safe sound exposure levels. This workshop introduces students to the innovative methods used by NMMF scientists and includes interactive activities that demonstrate the science of sound.

title: Marine Mammal Conservation Workshop
description:
The NMMF is a world leader in marine mammal conservation efforts, with programs helping endangered and threatened species such as bottlenose dolphins in the Gulf of Mexico, California Sea Lions, the critically endangered Vaquita porpoise, and the Southern Resident Killer Whales.



This program teaches basic marine mammal biology and ecology and highlights the exciting research of the NMMF. Most importantly, this program provides opportunities for young people in our community to engage with real STEAM professionals in Marine Mammal Science and teaches them what THEY can do to help!

title: Animal Helpers
description:
Animal Behavior and Training Workshop





The NMMF has partnered with the Girl Scouts of San Diego to teach local girls all about how animals help people and people help animals, with an emphasis on the special relationship that we have with dolphins, sea lions, and other marine mammals. We touch on marine mammal behavior and basic methods for training any type of animal.




Explore the connection between humans and animals



Find out how animals help keep people safe



Know how animals help people emotionally



Check out how animals help people with disabilities



Look at how animals might help us in the future












Animal Helpers Workshop Resources









Who Am I? Fun Facts About Animals

title: Under Pressure Workshop
description:
Discovering the Incredible Adaptations that Enable Marine Mammals to Explore the Ocean’s Depths



Marine Mammals share many traits with humans – they have large brains for their body size, they breathe air, and can become infected by similar diseases. However, one unique difference is that marine mammals are capable of deep diving by tolerating immense pressure. This workshop will focus on marine mammal adaptations to diving in the ocean and how they compare to human diving physiology.



Marine mammals are recognizable, charismatic animals. They are also some of the ocean’s long-lived top predators, and serve as sentinels of environmental health hazards in our oceans. This workshop is not only intended to educate the community about the impressive diving adaptations of marine mammals and introduce young people to the underwater world, but also to address the conservation issues of marine mammals, their applications to humans, and how each of us can help protect our shared oceans.

Register your K-6th graders today for a culinary adventure!

title: Talls & Smalls
description: Did you know that outdoor play has long-lasting benefits for both children and adults? Join us this month for Talls and Smalls, an outdoor adventure in the garden for ages 2-5 with an accompanying adult! Activities include: story time, a farm-to-table snack, a visit to one of our critters & time exploring the garden while tasting and learning together! Come spend quality time in nature with your little one while building a love for our environment and adventurous eating habits.

title: Talls and Smalls
description: Did you know that outdoor play has long-lasting benefits for both children and adults? Join us this month for Talls and Smalls, an outdoor adventure in the garden for ages 2-5 with an accompanying adult! Activities include: story time, a farm-to-table snack, a visit to one of our critters & time exploring the garden while tasting and learning together! Come spend quality time in nature with your little one while building a love for our environment and adventurous eating habits.

Of course, we will also make salsas and guacamole for topping!

This tour takes place on natural surfaces such as dirt and mulch so please wear appropriate shoes. Tours at 10:15AM & 11:15AM. No registration required.

title: Talls & Smalls June
description: Do you want your little one to form a love and curiosity around nature? Join us for this month’s Talls and Smalls adventure in the garden! Spend quality time exploring plants, animals and the environment with your little (ages 2-5). We can’t wait to see you! This class is funded in part, for a limited time, by First 5 San Diego.

title: Garden at Dusk Dinner with the Kitchenistas
description: Join us for the first Garden at Dusk Dinner of the year at Olivewood Gardens, a unique San Diego farm-to-table dinner event set beneath the stars. This special evening features an exciting collaboration between Olivewood Gardens and a local featured chef who will create a garden-inspired menu showcasing seasonal, locally sourced ingredients and their distinct culinary talents. Guests will enjoy a beautiful outdoor dining experience, curated beverages and live music to be announced soon. Tickets

title: Chefcitos
description: Celebrate Hispanic Heritage Month with us in the teaching kitchen! Chefcitos (grades K-6) will learn about this special celebration while making a variety of dishes from different countries including Mexico and El Salvador. Join us by registering today at olivewoodgardens.org!¡Celebra con nosotros el Mes de la Herencia Hispana en la cocina didáctica! Los Chefcitos (niños de los grados K-6 en la primaria) aprenderán sobre esta celebración especial mientras preparan una variedad de platos de difer

title: Youth Community Leadership Internship
description: Join us at Olivewood Gardens for an 8-week Youth Community Leadership Program! This program is for High School students who wants to gain skills and knowledge surrounding community engagement, food systems and social justice. The program culminates in a project proposal to create change in our community. Interns will: Learn about different aspects of their local food system, Learn about civic and community engagement, Create and engage in a community listening forum, Design and create a project

title: Garden Explorers April: Bugs!
description: Welcoming all K-6th graders to Olivewood Gardens. Spring time is filled with flowers and that means our pollinator friends are buzzing around! Sign your little one up for a morning of bug hunting and exploration! For Earth Month, you will also harvest and take home some yummy veggies! Saturday, April 11th 9:30am-11:30am.
