title: Voice Out 2024 – Outside The Lens
description: In May 2024, Outside The Lens launched its inaugural Voice Out: A Youth Media Arts Exhibition, showcasing the incredible talents of young artists from across the region. This year’s theme “PLACE,” inspired 118 submissions, each one a unique representation of what place means to our young creatives.

title: Outside The Lens presents Media Makers 2024 Winter Showcase – Outside The Lens
description: Join us for the Media Makers 2024 Winter Showcase presented by Outside The Lens!

title: Open House – Outside The Lens
description: Outside The Lens’ Open House is a vibrant celebration of creativity and community. Held in August at our state-of-the-art Wonder Lab in San Diego’s East Village, this event provides a unique opportunity to experience the transformative power of media arts.

title: Ignite – Outside The Lens
description: Ignite is an annual event hosted by Outside The Lens (OTL) to celebrate creativity, innovation, and transformation within San Diego’s vibrant artistic community.

title: Ignite: The Art of Reinvention – Outside The Lens
description: Join Outside The Lens in celebrating how storytelling and design drive creative transformation.

title: Land+Lenses: 1,000 Days – Outside The Lens
description: At this beachside workshop, you’ll set up an outdoor studio to document the impact of pollution and restricted access to public spaces. Through photography and sign-making, you’ll bring awareness to the effects of contamination from the Tijuana River and discuss the importance of land stewardship. Standing on a beach that has been closed for over […]

title: Land+Lenses: Landscape Dreams – Outside The Lens
description: Using a Polaroid camera, you’ll capture landscapes and overlay your images with personal reflections, drawings, or written intentions. Through guided prompts, you’ll explore your relationship with the land and dream about what an ideal park or community space could look like. At the end, you’ll contribute to a collective collage, creating a visual representation of […]

title: Land+Lenses: Park Not Dump – Outside The Lens
description: You’ll turn your focus to the park’s environment, exploring how contamination affects interconnected landscapes. You’ll discuss the relationship between land, water, and pollution while using photography and sign-making to advocate for environmental responsibility. This session encourages you to reclaim and reimagine public spaces through creative expression and activism. Come to a free Land and Lenses […]

title: Land+Lenses: Art In Action – Outside The Lens
description: In this kickoff session, you’ll get to know the Land + Lenses program, meet the facilitators, and explore the park through a hands-on scavenger hunt. Using cameras or drawing materials, you’ll document native plants and identify misplaced objects while engaging in a community clean-up. You’ll also have the chance to turn collected trash into creative […]

title: Land+Lenses: Nature in Focus – Outside The Lens
description: Discover how portraiture and nature photography can be powerful tools for environmental advocacy. You’ll learn photography techniques using DSLR cameras and experiment with capturing landscapes and portraits. Through discussion, you’ll reflect on your connection to nature and explore how images can be used to inspire change and bring awareness to environmental issues. Come to a […]

title: Land+Lenses: Planting Memories – Outside The Lens
description: In this hands-on workshop, you’ll create botanical prints on fabric or paper using native plants and cyanotype techniques. You’ll learn about photography’s connection to botanical documentation while reflecting on personal and cultural memories tied to the land. As you experiment with eco-printing, you’ll explore how nature can be both an artistic medium and a storyteller. […]

title: Land+Lenses: Portraits of Belonging – Outside The Lens
description: Step into a rotating outdoor photo studio where you’ll create portraits inspired by the four elements—earth, water, air, and fire. Each element represents themes like grounding, movement, and transformation. You’ll use props, explore different photography techniques, and reflect on your relationship to nature and belonging. By the end, you’ll have a series of portraits that […]

title: Land+Lenses: Sea & Story – Outside The Lens
description: Join a plein air painting session where you’ll paint the ocean and waves, contributing to a collective artwork that visually connects each participant’s piece. You’ll also record the sound of ocean waves, capturing movement and rhythm in both visual and auditory forms. This workshop invites you to reflect on the power of water, interconnectedness, and […]

title: Land+Lenses: Waves of Words – Outside The Lens
description: Breathwork, Poetry, and Open Mic… Immerse yourself in a creative space that blends deep breathing, poetry, and live music. You’ll participate in guided writing exercises, reflect on personal and collective experiences, and have the opportunity to share your words in an open mic setting. With the sounds of the ocean as a backdrop, this workshop […]

title: Voice Out 2025 – Outside The Lens
description: Outside the Lens invites you to Voice Out, its second annual juried youth media arts exhibition on May 3rd, 2025 from 4-7 p.m. at The Wonder Lab, a media arts hub in San Diego’s East Village. THIS EVENT celebrates the voices and stories of young artists from San Diego. OVER 100 ARTWORKS ranging from photography, illustration, mixed media and […]

title: Land+Lenses: Compost & Connect – Outside The Lens
description: Explore composting as a metaphor for transformation and healing. Guided by a guest artist, you’ll create compost dolls—biodegradable figures that can be planted or turned into chia pets. Through hands-on activities and discussion, you’ll reflect on cycles of life, grief, and renewal, considering how nature models regeneration through decay and growth. Come to a free […]

title: Land+Lenses: Rituals & Remedies – Outside The Lens
description: Engage your senses in an immersive exploration of herbalism and ritual. You’ll move through interactive stations, tasting locally foraged teas, crafting intention-based spell jars, making smudge bundles, and contributing to a community recipe book. As you work with plants, you’ll reflect on traditional knowledge, self-care, and your relationship with the land. Come to a free […]

title: Land+Lenses: Final Exhibition! – Outside The Lens
description: 📅 Saturday, May 31, 2025🕐 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM📍 The Front Arte y CulturaFree event | All ages welcome | Food provided Join us for the culminating exhibition of Land + Lenses, a community arts program that invited youth and families to explore the rich landscapes of Otay Valley Regional Park and YMCA Camp […]

title: Flashbacks: Film Photography & Alt. Photo Processes – Outside The Lens
description: San Diego Summer Camp 2025 by Outside The Lens at Liberty Station. Flashbacks: Film Photography & Alt. Photo Processes for Grades 6th-12th. Price: $300**Use Early Bird Discount CodeSUM25_DISCOUNT! at checkout$325 regular price after 4/15/25. Come and get retro with OTL! Discover some of photography's earliest techniques. Test out a 4x5 camera, capture & develop film, try out alternative photographic processes, and explore the past and present through the lens. Additional materials cost included

title: Photo Wonders: Trick Photography – Outside The Lens
description: Photo Wonders Trick Photography San Diego Summer Camp. Grades: 3rd-5th Cost: $275 with early bird discount codeSUM25_DISCOUNT! at checkout ($300 regular price)Transform your photos with creative techniques! Using iPads and DSLR cameras, develop your photography skills while creating eye-catching images as you explore special effects in photography including fun with double exposure, forced perspective, light writing, stop-motion animation and more.

title: Picture Perfect Photography Camp – Outside The Lens
description: San Diego Summer Camp 2025 with Outside the Lens for 3rd-5th grade. Embark on an exciting journey into the world of photography! Develop your photography skills and discover the wonders of capturing moments through the lens. Master camera basics, experiment with techniques, brainstorm creative ideas, capture and edit photos, and showcase your work! Equipment and snacks will be provided.

title: People and Places: The Art of Street Photography – Outside The Lens
description: San Diego Summer Camp 2025 by Outside The Lens at the La Jolla Historical Society. title: Capture and Create: Mixed Media Photography – Outside The Lens
description: San Diego Summer Camp from Outside The Lens for 6th to 8th Grade. Summer 2025. Capture and Create: Mixed Media Photography. Capture amazing photos, and transform them into unique pieces of artwork with the use of mixed media. Learn some new artistic techniques and add a new dimension to your photographic work!

title: Lights, Camera, Create: Narrative Filmmaking – Outside The Lens
description: San Diego Summer Camps at Outside The Lens. June 30, July 1, 2, 3, 2025 Time: 9:00am-3:30pm Grades: 6th-8th Location:Outside The Lens 125 14th Street San Diego, CA 92101 Price: $275 with early bird discount codeSUM25_DISCOUNT! at checkout $300 regular price. title: Mixed Media Snapshots – Outside The Lens
description: San Diego summer camp for 7th and 8th grades. Capture amazing photos, and transform them into unique pieces of artwork with the use of mixed media! Learn exciting new artistic techniques that add a new dimension to your photographic work! No prior experience necessary - just a passion for art and creating! This program is a collaboration of SDUSD, VAPA Foundation, and Outside The Lens.

title: Photo Transformations – Outside The Lens
description: For 7th and 8th grade students currently enrolled in SDUSD. Discover the power of photo editing to transform and manipulate images and create stunning visuals. In this hands-on camp, you will discover the fascinating world of photo editing and photo manipulation. Learn how to transform ordinary images into stunning visual stories using digital tools and techniques. No prior experience necessary - just bring your imagination and a desire to create. *This program is a collaboration of SDUSD, VAPA

title: Visualize Your Voice with Graphic Design – Outside The Lens
description: San Diego Summer Camp from Outside The Lens for 7th to 8th Grade. Summer 2025. Unleash your creativity and dive into the exciting world of design! This interactive camp will cover some of the fundamentals of graphic design, as well as exploring how design influences the world around you, and how you can use it to express your ideas and make an impact. title: Puente: Student Film Showcase – Outside The Lens
description: Join us for a powerful evening celebrating student voices, stories, and creativity! Students from the Puente Project at San Diego City College have spent the past 12 weeks working with Outside The Lens (OTL) to transform their poetry and lived experiences into original short films. Along the way, they’ve explored visual thinking, studied film techniques, […]

title: Open House 2025 – Outside The Lens
description: Come explore the creative world of Outside the Lens on August 28th! We're opening our doors to share how San Diego youth are using media arts to express themselves and change their communities. Join us for an evening of hands-on activities where you can create your own art alongside our young artists.

description:

Step into the glamour of the Roaring 20s, where the San Diego Automotive Museum comes alive with Art Deco elegance and the thrill of a bygone era. It’s an evening designed to delight the senses, spark conversation, and celebrate both the history of automotive innovation and the future it inspires.A 1920s ROARING AUTOMOTIVE AFFAIR

title: Dr. Perry Mansfield
description:
HONORINGDr. Perry MansfieldHIS MISSION
To inspire hope, expand the health and well-being of each patient with head and neck/skull base disease, by providing personalized optimal care through integrated clinical practice, and research.About Perry Mansfield MD, FRCSCChairman and Founder, The Mansfield CompaniesWe are truly honored to announce that Perry Mansfield, MD will be the honoree at this year’s THE Party in the Park—an evening dedicated to celebrating a man whose heart, generosity, and passion have left an indelible mark on our community.

For many in San Diego, Dr. Mansfield is first known as a compassionate and trusted physician. Throughout his distinguished medical career, he has cared for patients and families with empathy, integrity, and a steadfast commitment to excellence. His work reflects not only professional achievement, but a genuine desire to serve and uplift others.

Those who know him well also know that his love of automobiles runs just as deep. Dr.

title: The Ray Brock Hall of Fame | San Diego Automotive Museum
description:
Venue Rentals  >  The Hall of FameThe Ray Brock Hall of Fame
Elevate your next event—literally and figuratively—in our stunning Mezzanine. Overlooking the main exhibition floor, this airy, light-filled space offers a unique vantage point that immerses guests in the museum’s atmosphere while providing a sense of exclusivity and privacy.
With its open layout the mezzanine is ideal for receptions, cocktail gatherings, corporate events, intimate dinners, and special celebrations. Guests can mingle against a backdrop of artworks and exhibits below, enjoying a setting that blends sophistication, culture, and inspiration.
The space includes flexible seating and staging options, museum-quality lighting, and dedicated event support. Whether you’re planning an elegant evening event or a contemporary daytime gathering, the mezzanine provides a memorable setting that distinguishes your occasion from the ordinary.

title: The Grove | San Diego Automotive Museum
description:
Venue Rentals  >  The GroveThe Grove
The museum’s Grove offers a welcoming, scenic location for gatherings that feel relaxed yet special. Whether you’re planning a garden-style reception, a family celebration, or a community event, the space provides room to spread out, enjoy the fresh air, and connect in a setting inspired by art and nature.
With flexible arrangements and supportive staff, it’s the perfect place to bring people together. With the Cabrillo Bridge and view of Downtown San Diego, the Museum's Grove is sure to please.
The rental fee includes:

Dedicated museum staff
City permit (must be filed a minimum of 60 days prior)
Admission to the Museum.

A list of preferred vendors for rentals is available. REQUEST INFO NOW

title: The Exhibit Floor | San Diego Automotive Museum
description:
Venue Rentals  >  The Exhibit FloorThe Exhibit Floor
Step into a setting where art, technology, and history come together to create an unforgettable backdrop for your next event. Our Museum Exhibit Space offers a distinctive venue that blends architectural elegance with curated displays, providing guests with a one-of-a-kind experience the moment they arrive.
Designed to inspire conversation and spark imagination, this flexible gallery space accommodates a wide range of private events—including corporate receptions, intimate celebrations, lectures, fundraisers, and cocktail gatherings. Surrounded by thoughtfully arranged exhibits, guests can enjoy an immersive atmosphere that elevates any occasion.
Amenities include professional event support, museum-quality lighting, sound system and wi-fi. Whether you're hosting a sophisticated evening soirée or a daytime program with cultural flair, the Museum Exhibit Space offers a memorable setting that stands apart from traditional venues.

title: The Classroom or Boardroom | San Diego Automotive Museum
description:
Venue Rentals  >  The Classroom or BoardroomThe Classroom or Boardroom
Designed for productivity, collaboration, and comfort, our Classroom or Boardroom spaces offer flexible environments ideal for meetings, trainings, workshops, and small-group gatherings. Located within the museum, these rooms combine modern functionality with the inspiring atmosphere of a cultural setting.
The Boardroom features a polished, executive-style layout complete with a large conference table, ergonomic seating, three built in smart TVs, video conference ready, Keurig and cold beverages, perfect for leadership meetings, planning sessions, and private discussions. Your rental fee includes admission to the Museum.
The Classroom offers an adaptable setup suited for lectures, hands-on workshops, seminars, and educational programs. title: Events
description: Located in beautiful Balboa Park, The Nat features the natural history and unique biodiversity of Southern California and Baja California. No two visits are alike—there’s always something new to discover. Check out our daily schedule of activities, programs, films, and events.

title: Artist in Residence
description: The Nat’s new Artist in Residence Lisa Jetonne will be at the Museum creating participatory artworks for visitors to enjoy. Lisa’s work is inspired by the scientific research that takes place behind-the-scenes at the Museum and in the field daily and The Nat’s collection of more than 8 million specimens. Details on Lisa’s monthly activities can be found on our website.

title: Keynote Speaker
description: The Keynote Speaker for The State of Biodiversity, a symposium rallying around the latest science, emerging threats, and management success stories in the world of conservation.

title: Secret Society of Adultologists
description: Join this after-hours, 21+ event with entertainment, hands-on activities, and plenty of weird and wonderful natural science.

title: State of Biodiversity 2022
description: At the 2022 State of Biodiversity, we looked at how social issues, especially economic and racial inequities, shape the ecology and evolution of wildlife—particularly in mammals and carnivores. Social heterogeneity plays a pivotal role in shaping the ability of cities to support biodiversity. Thus, using a social-ecological and environmental justice lens is critical for climate and wildlife resilience in a human-dominated world.

				Taller de Compostaje Bilingüe »
			
			
		
	
	

			
			
			
			
						
				Celebrate World Jellyfish Day at the Living Coast!
Jellyfish, contrary to their name, are invertebrates that use a form of jet propulsion to move through the water. They have been around for millions of years, moving through the ocean’s current. Although they are feared by most because of their sting, jellyfish play an important role in our marine ecosystem. They are a key source of food for sea turtles and other animals, and even double as protection for other species, like oysters, from predators!
Visit the Living Coast on November 4 & 5 to celebrate this fascinating specie

				February 2 is World Wetlands Day! Did you know that the Living Coast is located on the San Diego Bay National Wildlife Refuge? 				Join us on April 13 & 14 to celebrate International Bat Appreciation Day with educational Discovery Tables, arts & crafts, and animal encounters!
International Bat Appreciation Day is April 17. This holiday reminds us of the vital role that bats play in our daily lives. Did you know that plants such as bananas, mangoes, cashews, dates, avocadoes, peaches, cloves, and figs (to name a few) rely on bats for pollination? Bats also help distribute the seeds of these plants, so they can reproduce and create more fruit for us humans to eat and enjoy. Join us for a bilingual (English/Spanish) birding hike as we explore the Tijuana Estuary! The Tijuana River National Estuarine Research Reserve is a marsh land located in the most southwestern part of the United States. The flat trail takes you through the reserve and along the Imperial Beach coastline, with the Mexican border in the background. Help us look for local bird species and contribute to the 2024 Border BioBlitz as we encounter other native wildlife and plants in this unique and endangered ecosystem.
